meta.description: Batch 3 QA and verification pass for NORS Crosswalk Tool. Contains: (1) appendix_pp_page_rows with all ~90 F-tags corrected to Rev. 232 PDF page numbers; (2) keyword_rows for all 41 Batch 2 NORS codes; (3) human_review_flags for F553/F801 no-header tags, medication distinctions, F627/F628 confirmation, Rev. 232 page corrections, and A01+F602 mapping correction; (4) do_not_map_catalog for deleted tags, reserved tags, outside-agency codes, and medication chain. No code_summary_rows or authority_rows in this batch — those are in Batch 1 and Batch 2.

do_not_map_catalog.reason: F758 was deleted in Rev. 229 (04-28-25). All psychotropic/chemical restraint content was absorbed into F605. F758 no longer exists in the current Appendix PP (Rev. 232). Any citation of F758 is invalid.
do_not_map_catalog.correct_mapping_for_ftag: F758 content → F605 (current active tag for psychotropics/chemical restraints)

do_not_map_catalog.reason: F757 (Unnecessary Drugs — General, §483.45(d)) is NOT the same as F605 (Psychotropic Drugs). F12 (Chemical Restraint = drug used for discipline/convenience) maps primarily to F605, not F757. F757 covers all unnecessary drugs (any drug without adequate indication), which is a broader category. Do NOT substitute F757 for F605 in chemical restraint cases.
do_not_map_catalog.correct_mapping_for_ftag: F757 maps to F12 as related (secondary), not as direct primary.

do_not_map_catalog.reason: F556 is RESERVED. No CFR section is assigned. No citations should ever be made under F556. Any automated tool logic that might suggest F556 must be blocked.

K01-K06 series codes represent complaints about OUTSIDE AGENCIES (regulatory system, Medicaid, managed care, Medicare, VA, private insurance). These complaints are NOT facility deficiencies and must never be mapped to Appendix PP F-tags. Route exclusively to the relevant agency plus ombudsman authority (45 CFR §1324.19).

L01-L03 series codes represent NON-FACILITY SYSTEM complaints (family/representative conflict, outside provider services, non-facility community transition barriers). These are not facility deficiencies and must never be mapped to Appendix PP F-tags. Exception: if facility inaction is a contributing factor in L03, may route to §483.15(b). Route to ombudsman authority (45 CFR §1324.13, §1324.19) and appropriate community resources.

No transportation-specific F-tag exists in Appendix PP. Transportation complaints (G02) should not be mapped to any survey F-tag. Route to §483.10(f)(1) self-determination advisory and Medicaid NEMT benefit. Do NOT cite F579 (telephone) or other communication tags for transportation issues.
correct_ftags_for_nors_code: F561 (self-determination — advisory only, not a direct citation)

do_not_map_catalog.reason: G03 (Conflict Resolution) is a pure ombudsman mediation role. No survey F-tag is appropriate for resident-on-resident conflict resolution by ombudsman. Only if conflict creates safety risk should F584 be considered for referral to surveyor, not as an ombudsman citation.
correct_ftags_for_nors_code: F584 (surveyor referral only if safety risk, not ombudsman citation)

ombudsman_tips: Resident has right to inspect and copy records within 24 hours (business days) under §483.10(g)(2)
Request must be responded to in reasonable timeframe
If denied, document date, who denied, and reason given
Cross-reference F573 in Appendix PP for surveyor guidance
§483.10(b)(1) requires facility to inform resident of rights in understandable language and format
If resident has LEP (limited English proficiency), facility must provide interpreter or translated materials
Document specific language need and whether facility has a policy
Related to Title VI civil rights obligations for Medicaid-funded facilities
45 CFR §1324.11 guarantees ombudsman access to facilities, residents, and records
Willful interference with ombudsman is separately reportable under state law
If facility actor — cite F586 (§483.10(k) contact with external entities)
If non-facility actor — route to 45 CFR §1324.11(a)(1)(i) only; no F-tag for non-facility interference
Document exact nature of interference, staff/party involved, and date
§483.15(a) prohibits requiring third-party guarantee of payment as condition of admission
Admission agreements cannot waive federal/state rights
If resident was admitted against their wishes, this may also implicate A-series abuse codes
Review admission agreement for prohibited clauses — document specific language
F620 governs admissions policy in Appendix PP
§483.15(c)(5) requires facility to inform resident of right to appeal a transfer/discharge
Medicaid residents have right to request state fair hearing to contest discharge
Ombudsman can assist resident in filing appeal or understanding process
F627/F628 (Rev. 229) govern transfer/discharge process and documentation
Time limits apply — document date notice was received
§483.10(e)(7) gives resident right to refuse intra-facility transfer if purpose is convenience/cost
If room change is for a legitimate clinical reason, facility must provide notice
Spousal room-sharing right is protected under F559
Document whether notice was given, reason stated, and resident's response
F560 (§483.10(e)(7)) is the primary F-tag for room change refusal
§483.15(b)(1) requires facility to assist residents who wish to return to/remain in community
Ombudsman may refer to Money Follows the Person or similar transition programs
Document if facility failed to initiate transition planning when requested
If transition barriers are NOT caused by facility, use L03 instead
APS/protective services referral if transition is being blocked by family or representative
§483.10(f)(5) protects right to organize and participate in resident/family groups
Facility must provide space, privacy, staff assistance, and respond to council concerns
Staff may not interfere with or dominate council activities
Document specific interference: was it room denial, staff attendance, or failure to respond?
F565 in Appendix PP covers resident group rights
No direct F-tag covers billing disputes per se
F567-F570 cover resident trust fund/financial management, not billing errors
Route to CT DMAS, CMS, or managed care plan for coverage disputes
Billing errors related to trust fund mismanagement may implicate F602
Document itemized charges and what resident disputes
F602 applies when facility or facility staff misappropriate resident property
If perpetrator is family or outside visitor — route to Adult Protective Services (APS)
Trust fund: facility must maintain separate accounts and provide quarterly statements
Document items, approximate value, when noticed missing, and who had access
This is one of the few codes where F602 (§483.12 financial exploitation) is directly applicable
§483.25(d) requires adequate supervision and assistive devices to prevent accidents
F689 in Appendix PP covers accident prevention — start at page 350 in Rev. 232
Review care plan for fall risk assessment and interventions documented
Bed rails are separately regulated under F700 (§483.25(n))
Document circumstances: time, location, witnesses, injury, care plan in place
§483.24(a) and §483.25 require ADL assistance commensurate with resident's assessed needs
F676/F677 cover ADL assistance including bathing, grooming, dressing
Resident has right to choose bathing schedule and method within care plan
Document frequency of bathing offered/refused, hygiene status observed, and care plan
Odor or soiled clothing observed during visit is documentable evidence
§483.10(d) protects right to choose attending physician and access healthcare services
§483.25 requires facility to provide necessary care based on resident's comprehensive assessment
If facility failed to act on change in condition, also consider F07 or F684
Document timeline: when change noticed, when family/resident notified, when physician contacted
F555 covers choice of physician; F684 covers quality of care generally
§483.25 requires facility to identify and address changes in resident condition
§483.10(g)(14)/F580 requires timely notification of significant condition changes to family
Document specific symptoms, when reported, response time, and outcome
If symptom was pain — may also implicate quality of care (F684) and care plan (F656)
Pressure ulcers, infections, and dehydration are common unattended symptoms
§483.25(e) requires facility to provide bladder and bowel management based on assessment
F690 in Appendix PP (page 375 Rev. 232) covers incontinence care in detail
Resident cannot be left in wet/soiled briefs for extended period — dignity violation
Toileting program must be in care plan if resident is incontinent
UTIs that recur may signal inadequate catheter care or toileting program
§483.25(h) and §483.10(b) protect resident's right to necessary assistive devices
Facility must ensure devices are maintained, available, and functioning
Loss of hearing aid or glasses is a property complaint (E02) as well as care complaint
Document specific device, its condition, date last seen, and resident impact
F688 (mobility/ROM) and F689 (accidents) may also apply if device absence caused injury
§483.24(a) requires facility to assist resident in maintaining/improving function
F676/F677/F688 cover functional maintenance and ADL assistance
Medicare Part A SNF benefit covers skilled rehabilitation — denial may be appeals issue
Restorative nursing program (less than skilled therapy) must be in care plan
If therapy was reduced without clinical justification, document and consider surveyor referral
§483.10(e) protects right to be free from physical restraints used for discipline or convenience
F700 (bed rails) and F605 (chemical restraints) are closely related F-tags
Any physical device that restricts movement requires written order and informed consent
Restraint must be least restrictive option; alternatives must be documented and tried
Ombudsman should ask: Is there an order? Was consent obtained? Were alternatives tried?
§483.10(e) and §483.45 prohibit using drugs as restraints for discipline or convenience
F605 (page 130 Rev. 232) covers psychotropic drugs including antipsychotics
F757 (page 616 Rev. 232) covers unnecessary drugs generally — broader than just psychotropics
Key distinction: F12 = chemical restraint (discipline/convenience); F605 = any psychotropic per §483.45(e)
GDR (gradual dose reduction) attempts are required unless clinically contraindicated
Informed consent required for psychotropic medications in Connecticut
§483.80 requires a comprehensive infection prevention and control program (IPCP)
F880 (page 809 Rev. 232) is the primary F-tag for infection control
Outbreak reporting requirements exist at state level (CT DPH)
Document: number of residents/staff affected, PPE availability, facility response
Infection control failures may also implicate quality of care (F684) and staffing (F725)
§483.24(c) requires ongoing program of activities designed to meet residents' interests
F679/F680 in Appendix PP cover activities program and director qualifications
Activities must reflect individual resident preferences from assessment
Posted activities schedule must be followed or residents notified of cancellation
Resident right to participate or refuse activities without retaliation
No specific F-tag exists for transportation; ombudsman assists under §483.10(f)(1) self-determination
Medicaid non-emergency medical transportation (NEMT) is a covered benefit — contact state Medicaid
Facility obligation is to assist in arranging (not necessarily providing) transportation
If lack of transportation prevents access to healthcare, also consider F06
CT DSS administers Medicaid NEMT benefit
G03 is a pure ombudsman mediation role — no survey F-tag citation is appropriate
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1)
If behavior rises to resident-on-resident abuse, escalate to A-series and alert surveyor agency
Document: nature of conflict, parties, prior attempts at resolution, and current status
Facility has obligation under F584 (safe environment) if conflict creates safety risk
§483.40(d) and F745 require medically-related social services based on assessment
Social services must help resident attain/maintain health and well-being
Facilities with 120+ beds must have qualified social worker on staff
Document specific unmet need and whether resident/family has requested services
Advance directives (F578) and healthcare proxy may be handled through social services
§483.60(e) requires therapeutic diets per physician order — F808 (page 672 Rev. 232)
§483.60(d) requires palatable food that meets food preferences — F804-F807
Resident has right to choose food alternatives within acceptable nutritional parameters
Document specific diet order, what was served, and how it differed from the order
Food allergy violations are both a care issue (F684) and a therapeutic diet issue (F808)
§483.90(i) requires safe/comfortable environment — F921-F926 cover specific environmental conditions
F922 (water), F923 (ventilation) apply to specific temperature/air complaints
F584 (safe environment) also applies if temperature extremes pose health risk
CT regulations may specify temperature ranges — cross-reference CT DPH facility standards
Document specific temperature readings, times, and resident health impact
§483.90 covers physical environment — F920-F926 are the primary F-tags
F584 (safe environment) is relevant if building hazard creates safety risk
Lighting inadequacy is specifically addressed under F921 environmental conditions
Document location, nature of hazard, when reported to facility, and response
Life safety code violations should be referred to CT DPH fire/life safety inspector
§483.90 and §483.10 protect resident's right to adequate supplies and furnishings
F584 (safe environment) applies if broken furnishings create a safety risk
Personal property storage: facility must provide secure storage for valuables (F567-F570 area)
Supply shortages that affect care quality may implicate J02 (fiscal management)
Document specific items missing/broken, date first noticed, and facility response
No single ADA/accessibility F-tag exists in nursing home survey — use F920-F926 range
F924 (handrails in corridors) is the most specific environmental accessibility tag
ADA complaints may be routed to HHS Office for Civil Rights or CT CHRO
Physical accessibility within the facility is also a quality-of-life issue (F675)
Document specific barrier, resident need, and impact on daily life
§483.60(i) and §483.90 cover sanitation and pest control — F812/F813/F925
F812 (food safety) applies if kitchen/food storage has pest problems
F813 (pest control/sanitation) applies to general facility pest complaints
F925 specifically covers pest control program
Housekeeping deficiencies that create health risks may also implicate F880 (infection control)
Document type of pest, location observed, date, and facility response
No single 'administrator absent' F-tag; cite §483.70 (administration) generally
Falsified records may implicate multiple F-tags including F600-F610 (abuse/investigation)
Background screening failures: 42 CFR §483.70(e) requires criminal background checks
If records are falsified or missing, document what records and what was expected
Refer to CT DADS for licensing violations if facility-level governance is the issue
No direct 'fiscal crisis' F-tag; cite resulting care failures (F725 staffing, F800-F813 nutrition, etc.)
Facility closure notification requirements: §483.15(c) requires advance notice to residents
Report imminent closure risk to CT DADS and CMS immediately
Staff alerts of not being paid is a sentinel indicator — document and escalate
Coordinate with CT DADS on emergency placement if closure is imminent
K01 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: CT DPH Survey Division for nursing home licensing/survey complaints
CMS complaint: 1-800-MEDICARE or CMS.gov complaint portal
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1) for complaint resolution
Ombudsman may help resident navigate the survey/complaint process
K02 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: CT DSS (Department of Social Services) for Medicaid eligibility issues
Medicaid fair hearing rights: resident may request hearing within 90 days of denial
Legal aid may assist with Medicaid appeals — provide referral
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1)
K03 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: CT DSS Managed Care Operations or the specific managed care plan
Resident has right to file internal MCO grievance and external appeal
CT Office of Healthcare Advocate can assist with managed care complaints
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1)
K04 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
SNF Medicare appeal (BFCC-QIO): 1-888-319-8452 (Livanta for CT)
Expedited appeal must be requested before discharge from Medicare-covered stay
Medicare drug coverage complaints: Part D plan or SHIP counselor
CT SHIP (1-800-994-9422) provides free Medicare counseling
K05 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: local VA regional office or CT Veterans Affairs (portal.ct.gov/DVA)
County Veterans Service Officers (CVSO) can assist with VA benefits claims
Aid and Attendance pension benefit helps veterans pay for nursing home care
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1)
K06 is an outside agency complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: CT Insurance Department (ct.gov/cid) for insurance company complaints
Long-term care insurance disputes: document denial letter and policy terms
CT Insurance Department consumer helpline can mediate insurance complaints
Ombudsman authority: 45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1)

keywords: family making decisions against resident wishes
ombudsman_tips: L01 is a non-facility system complaint — no direct F-tag applicable
Ombudsman's role is to advocate for resident's expressed wishes and self-determination
If representative conduct rises to exploitation or abuse, elevate to A04/F602 and APS
Guardianship/conservatorship disputes require probate court involvement — refer to legal aid
45 CFR §1324.13 and §1324.19 govern ombudsman advocacy with representatives
L02 is a non-facility system complaint — no facility F-tag applicable
Route to: the licensing authority for the specific provider type
If outside provider is hospice — route to CT DPH Hospice licensure
If outside provider arranged by facility — may implicate facility oversight duties
Document provider name, service type, nature of complaint, and date
L03 covers transition barriers NOT caused by facility — no facility F-tag applicable
If barriers are caused by facility inaction, use D02 instead (facility obligation)
Route to: CT DSS LTSS, Money Follows the Person (MFP) program, or community resources
ADRC (Aging and Disability Resource Center) can assist with community placement
45 CFR §1324.19(a)(1) authorizes ombudsman assistance with transition planning

human_review_flags.description: F801 (§483.60(a) Qualified Dietary Staff) has no standalone Rev. header in Rev. 232 PDF. Content is embedded within the §483.60(a) section between F800 (page 660) and F802 (page 663). Best-available page assignment: 660 with caveat. Surveyor guidance for F801 is integrated with F800/F802 text.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must display note: 'F801 shares §483.60(a) section with F800/F802 in Rev. 232 PDF (pages 660-663). No standalone surveyor guidance page exists. Reference page 660 with caveat.' CODEX: set appendix_pp_pdf_page_start=660 with notes field populated.

human_review_flags.description: F553 (§483.10(c)(2)-(3) Right to Participate in Planning Care) has no standalone Rev. header in Rev. 232 PDF. Content begins on page 15 within the F552 section (§483.10(c)). The F553 tag appears embedded under the §483.10(c)(2)-(3) subsection on page 15.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must display note: 'F553 shares §483.10(c) section with F552 in Rev. 232 PDF. Content starts page 15. No standalone surveyor guidance header.' CODEX: set appendix_pp_pdf_page_start=15 with notes field populated.

human_review_flags.description: Medication F-tags must be distinguished carefully. The correct ladder (most specific to least specific): (1) F759/F760 = medication administration errors (§483.45(f)); (2) F757 = unnecessary drugs — general (§483.45(d)) — ACTIVE, confirmed Rev. 232 page 616; (3) F605 = psychotropic drugs / chemical restraints (§483.10(e)/§483.45(e)) — also absorbed F758; (4) F756 = drug regimen review (§483.45(c)) — pharmacist review failures; (5) F755 = pharmacy services — general (§483.45) — broadest catch-all. CRITICAL: F758 was DELETED in Rev. 229 and all content moved to F605. F757 is ACTIVE and should NOT be confused with F758.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Tool must use the following routing logic for medication complaints: Medication error (wrong drug/dose/time/person) → F759/F760. Drug used for discipline/convenience → F605 (chemical restraint). Antipsychotic without indication/GDR → F605. Unnecessary drug (any drug without adequate indication) → F757. Pharmacist review failure → F756. NEVER cite F758 — redirect to F605. CODEX: add routing logic note to all medication-related NORS codes (F12, F07, F11).

human_review_flags.description: F627 and F628 were added in Rev. 229 (replacing F622-F626, F660, F661). Both have been independently confirmed in Rev. 232 PDF: F627 at page 197 (§483.15(c) Transfer and Discharge); F628 at page 219 (§483.15(c)(2) Documentation). Any Batch 2 authority rows that cited F627/F628 as 'page null' should be updated to reflect the confirmed Rev. 232 pages.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: CODEX: Update all authority rows and appendix_pp_page_rows that contain F627 or F628 to set appendix_pp_pdf_page_start = 197 (F627) and 219 (F628). Remove 'relocated — page null' notes. Add 'Rev. 229 addition, confirmed Rev. 232' note.

human_review_flags.description: All appendix_pp_page_rows in Batch 2 were based on Rev. 225 PDF (08-08-24, 873 pages). The live production PDF is now Rev. 232 (07-23-25, ~930 pages). Page numbers have shifted for most F-tags due to Rev. 229 content additions (F627/F628 Transfer/Discharge sections, updated F725/F726 Nursing Services). Batch 3 appendix_pp_page_rows provides corrected Rev. 232 pages for all ~90 F-tags.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: CODEX: Replace all appendix_pp_page_rows from Batch 2 with Batch 3 corrected rows. Update pdf_revision field from 'Rev. 225' to 'Rev. 232 (07-23-25)' on all rows. Key page differences (Rev.225 → Rev.232): F627: null → 197; F628: null → 219; F675: 264 → 284; F689: 326 → 350; F740: 501 → 548; F880: 770 → 809; F920: 848 → 900.

human_review_flags.description: Escalation of Batch 2 HRF-006. The existing crosswalk_catalog.json maps A01 (Physical Abuse) to F602. This is INCORRECT. F602 covers §483.12 financial misappropriation/exploitation ONLY. A01 maps to F600 (direct), F607 (related), F609 (related). F602 correctly maps to A04 (Financial Exploitation) and E02 (Personal Property Loss). This error must be corrected in the live tool before Batch 3 data is integrated.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: CODEX: (1) Remove F602 from A01 mappings in crosswalk_catalog.json. (2) Add F600 as direct mapping for A01. (3) Verify A04 authority rows include F602 as direct. (4) Verify E02 authority rows include F602 as direct. This is a data integrity correction that affects the live tool.
